title: Factory Tutorial Part 1   Oscillators
description:
Factory's Oscillators.

Index:
00:29 Pulse Sync
00:50 Saw Sync
01:00 Saw Fractal
01:31 FM Formant
02:35 The Transformer
03:50 Wavetable Modes (PWM, Sync, Formant & Drone)
06:24 Waveguide
07:17 Modulating transformer position

Factory is the new poly-synth from Sugar Bytes.

http://www.sugar-bytes.de/content/products/Factory/index.php?lang=en

More Videos:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lfYZk8Fn4-A

transcript: [Music] [Applause] what's up everybody you're watching a series on sugar bites Factory my name's Tom km and I'm going to be showing you the key features of this awesome synth and some unique ways to use them today's video I just want to focus on the oscillators we've got two oscillators here OS one and Os 2 they're completely identical however using this drop- down menu here we can change how the oscillators function so to start off with we have a pulse sync this is your basic pulse or Square wave we can combine several pulse waves sync with each other with a sync freak we have a pulse width and there's also a key track in case you want to change the syn freak based on the note that you're playing moving on to the sour sync this is similar to the pulse sync we have a sync frequency and we have a key track nice basic Source stuff the next one is interesting it's called a sore fractal now this is a saw tooth that gets chopped into fragments at a set rate and then that's fed back into the oscillator really good for bright shimmery sounds I'm just going to play a note and move the fractalize knob for you space hyperdrive stuff and the shape knob determines whether it's a pulse harmonic if you bring it to the left or a saw to the right lots of fun the next one on the list is an FM format now this is a phase modulation function it has a sine wave modulated by a second sine wave and we have these four options here so the FM index controls the amount of phase modulation the feedback enriches the wave with harmonics and it goes all the way up to a Sawtooth we have a fourment shift and then we have a ratio to get much higher harsher harmonics really really good for screaming sounds or pive sounds and keep in mind that the ratio is completely scaled so everything is harmonic uh we get no dissonance when we're playing notes or chords with this particular function okay moving on we have the Transformer now I love this one you can load your own complex samples into this area here and what it does is it analyzes it and chops it up into individual little Loops that it can play so it's kind of a granular type oscillator um for an example let's use a let's go vocal choir and here we have a sample and you'll see it's loaded up here if I play a not on my keyboard and move the position it's going to move through the complex waveform and loop individual segments of it we also have a format control which is good another neat thing you can do with this is I'm going to choose a vocal and I'm going to go synthesizer here I'm just going to play a single bring the format up play a single note and move the position bring the format down make it low so there's a lot of fun you can have with this um I'm going to show you more about this at the end of the video and show you a cool technique to get this really talking the next one on the list is the wave table pwm so this is wave table pulse with modulation uh sugar bites have given us a whole bunch of different wave tables that we can select from here uh let's choose something I like these vocalist ones we'll go animal and we can cycle through the wave table with this wave knob let's make the note higher we also have a pulse word so nice kind of basic wave table synthesis going on here the next one on the list is a wave table sync you notice we have four different wave tables here they're all quite similar but have unique functions the wave table sync allows us to sync the wave to another wave running at a higher frequency so basically we've got a sync freak like we did with the pulse and the soore but in a wave table so let me just play a note and cycle through the wave table for you now as I bring this up it's going to sink it with a wave which is much higher lot of fun the next one on the list again another wave table this one is a wave table format so we have a cycling of a waveform here or of the wave table sorry and we also have a format control let's pick a different wave table here uh we'll go sign harmonics but now we can change the format shift the next one is called a wave table drone now this is great for all you ambient or drone freaks out there again wave table synthesis we can cycle through everything here or through the wave table but we have a jet knob and it it creates a cluster of processed wave tables really changing the sound really creepy stuff sometimes so let's play I'm just going to play a note and move the jit knob let's play a higher note and this sounds really good with something like a white verb let's turn that on give it maximum maximum mix real horror movie stuff so that's quite a lot of fun finally we have the wave guide now this is good for kind of keys or percussion or something plucked for example uh it combines the wave table with a resonator style algorithm basically so I'm going to show you a few examples here let's just go into my instruments and I'm going to choose piano let's bring the dampening down to about 3/4 and let's just play some keys should probably turn the white verb off for the example so it's also really good for B sounds so let's go and change the instrument to a base I'm going to bring the dampening down below halfway and uh yeah so there we go that's the wave guide just to finish I want to show you a cool thing you can do with the Transformer this was actually shown to me by one of the developers at sugar bites and I thought it was pretty cool so I've gone ahead and done this myself um previously I recorded myself Whispering the word Factory into the microphone I saved this as a mono file in the vocal folder in the samples folder of the factory presets and that means I can now access it in the drop- down menu so I go vocal Factory whisper here it is here I'm just going to play the note and move the position knob but I'm going to bring the tuning down to 36 now that's still good but what we can do is we can modulate this position knob using the modulation Matrix so there's two ways we could do this we could use an envelope or we could use a sequencer I'm going to start with an envelope so from this menu I'm going to select oscillator 1B which is this position knob and I'm going to give it positive amounts on envelope 2 so that means over in our envelopes here envelope 2 is now controlling this position B knob and I'm going to bring the attack up quite a lot on the envelope so now when I play this note it says it for me if I bring it shorter really short we can even make it go backwards so we could bring the position up to the top make this a negative value excellent now so that's with the envelope but I want to do it with a sequence so let's use sequence one give it a positive value go over to my sequences make sure sequence one is selected and I'm going to use this particular step function here and then click click and drag it across so all the steps are the same this means I can now draw a custom envelope so I'm going to just have it sloping up all the way to the end that's good enough and then let's play oh make sure the position knob is back to its minimum value and you can play with these to capture various Loops of that complex wave but what I'm going to do is right at the end here I'm going to make these all drag out I'm going to make this last step a whole value so it completely just doesn't slope down at the end and I'm going to make it a one shot that means when the sequences finished playing it's going to stick on this last step and of course let's give it some Reverb so a lot of fun to bee with that you can take vocals you can mck things up you can you can even do things like uh um use the oscillator that this is on the Transformer the complex wave and you can use the oscillator OS one to modulate something else but I won't get into that for now so that's the oscillators in a nutshell I really hope you've enjoyed this and stay tuned for the next video
